title: Strength in China's Coal Imports Still Very Much Needed to Offset Moderate Fleet Growth
section_path: Strength in China's Coal Imports Still Very Much Needed to Offset Moderate Fleet Growth
section_path_text: Strength in China's Coal Imports Still Very Much Needed to Offset Moderate Fleet Growth
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
Last week was another good week in the dry bulk spot market, although the 1% growth shown with the publication of China's March coal-derived electricity generation data did come as a surprise to us. This is an area that we will be continuing to monitor closely and will be continuing to publish updates on for subscribers of Commodore's Global Dry Bulk Research Subscription. Image reference: Chart 1.jpg | assets/2024-04-26_strength-in-chinas-coal-imports-still-very-much-needed-to-offset-moderate-fleet_img_chart1_9e40dedbbd86.jpg
Not coming as a surprise to us, however, and remaining helpful for the dry bulk market, has been that China's coal production has continued to contract on a year-on-year basis by 4% due to the ongoing safety overhaul that we have continued to examine in our work.
